title: Recent sessions section - Cross-directory recent list
description:
The iOS app has a collapsible 'Recent' section in DirectoryListView showing the N most recent sessions across ALL directories. This provides quick access to recently used sessions without navigating into each directory.

## iOS Implementation
- DirectoryListView.swift lines 153-176
- Shows session name, directory (last path component), and relative timestamp
- Configured via recent_sessions_limit setting
- Receives data from backend via recent_sessions WebSocket message

## Acceptance Criteria
- Add collapsible Recent section above directory list
- Show recent sessions from all directories
- Display: session name, directory name (last component), relative time
- Tap navigates to conversation view
- Respect recent-sessions-limit setting

title: API Key management view with manual entry
description:
iOS has APIKeyManagementView for manual API key entry and validation. RN auth view only shows QR scanner option.

**iOS Features:**
- Manual text input field for API key
- Real-time validation (format: untethered-[32 hex chars])
- Key deletion with confirmation dialog
- Keychain-based secure storage
- Connection test before saving
- Clear error messages for invalid keys

**Required for RN:**
1. Create API key entry form in auth view
2. Add validation for key format
3. Integrate with react-native-keychain for secure storage
4. Add connection test functionality
5. Show clear error/success states

**Files to modify:**
- frontend/src/voice_code/views/auth.cljs - Add manual entry form

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Text input for API key with proper masking
- [ ] Format validation with error message
- [ ] Connection test before saving key
- [ ] Key stored in secure storage (Keychain)
- [ ] Delete key option with confirmation

title: [UI Bug] Navigation crash - Cannot read property navigate of undefined
description:
## Error
Uncaught Error: Cannot read property 'navigate' of undefined

## Severity
SYSTEMIC - affects nearly all navigation actions. Occurs repeatedly (Log 4 of 4, Log 5 of 5 observed).

## Call Stack (partial)
- loadModuleImplementation (require.js:238)
- _performTransitionSideEffects (Pressability.js:758)
- _receiveSignal (Pressability.js:697)
- responderEventHandlers.onResponderRelease

## Likely Cause
Something is calling navigate() when nav-ref is undefined. Possibilities:
1. A useEffect or subscription callback tries to navigate before NavigationContainer mounts
2. Hot reload invalidates the nav-ref but components still hold stale reference
3. Error boundary or toast component tries to navigate on error

## Reproduction
Almost any navigation action triggers this:
- Navigate to SessionList with empty params
- Navigate to CommandExecution
- General navigation flow

## Impact
Blocks normal app usage - error overlay appears frequently
close_reason: Closed

title: Draft message persistence
description:
iOS uses DraftManager to persist draft messages per-session to UserDefaults with 300ms debounce. RN drafts stored only in volatile app-db.

**iOS DraftManager Features:**
- Per-session draft storage (session ID -> text)
- Debounced writes to UserDefaults (300ms)
- save(), get(), clear(), cleanup() lifecycle
- Survives app restarts and background

**Current RN State:**
- Drafts in app-db under :ui :drafts
- Lost on app restart
- No debouncing on writes

**Implementation:**
1. Add draft persistence to AsyncStorage
2. Debounce writes (300ms) to reduce I/O
3. Load drafts on app init
4. Clear draft on message send
5. Cleanup stale drafts periodically

**Files to modify:**
- frontend/src/voice_code/persistence.cljs - Add draft persistence
- frontend/src/voice_code/events/core.cljs - Add draft save effect

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Draft text survives app restart
- [ ] Drafts debounced to reduce I/O
- [ ] Draft cleared after successful send
- [ ] Old drafts for deleted sessions cleaned up

title: [UI Bug] Persistent REPL exception toast errors
description:
## Symptom
Toast showing 'REPL Invoke Exception Error: No protocol m...' with incrementing badge count

## Behavior
- Persists across all screens
- Obscures voice buttons on Conversation screen
- Badge count increments over time

## Likely Cause
ClojureScript protocol errors bubbling up to error toast system
close_reason: Development-time shadow-cljs REPL error display, not a production code bug. Toasts appear when REPL evaluation fails during development.

title: Android Build Verification
description:
Verify the app builds and runs correctly on Android.

Required:
- Build APK successfully
- Test on Android emulator
- Verify WebSocket connectivity
- Test all screens and functionality
- Fix any Android-specific issues

title: [Infra] iOS simulator can't connect to Metro bundler
description:
The iOS simulator shows 'No script URL provided' error when trying to load the React Native app.

**Symptoms:**
- App shows red screen with 'No script URL provided'
- unsanitizedScriptURLString = (null)

**Investigation:**
- Metro is running on port 8081 and serving bundles correctly (verified via curl)
- shadow-cljs is running and compiling
- The issue is network connectivity between simulator and localhost

**Potential causes:**
- RCTBundleURLProvider settings
- Simulator network configuration
- Need to use machine IP instead of localhost

**Workaround:**
May need to configure the bundle URL in Debug menu or use the machine's IP address.
close_reason: iOS simulator successfully connects to Metro bundler - app displays auth screen correctly

title: [UI Bug] Unsafe navigation calls - missing nil checks
description:
## Problem
Multiple views call .navigate on the navigation prop without checking if it exists.

## Affected Files
- directory_list.cljs: settings-button uses (.navigate navigation ...) without guard
- session_list.cljs: commands-button and render-item use (.navigate navigation ...) without guard  
- command_menu.cljs: command on-press handlers use (.navigate navigation ...) without guard

## Root Cause
The navigation prop can be undefined during:
- Hot reload (stale references)
- Component mounting race conditions
- Module loading

## Fix
Wrap all .navigate calls:
```clojure
(when navigation
  (.navigate navigation "ScreenName" params))
```

Or use the safe navigate! function from voice-code.views.core:
```clojure
(voice-code.views.core/navigate! "ScreenName" params)
```

## Related
This is the root cause of VCMOB-1i4 (navigation crash)
close_reason: Closed

title: Active session tracking for smart TTS
description:
iOS has ActiveSessionManager to track which session is visible. Used for smart TTS - only auto-speaks responses for the active session.

**iOS Features:**
- Singleton tracking current visible session
- isActive(sessionId) check in speak logic
- Only auto-speaks if user is viewing that session
- Prevents surprise TTS from background sessions

**Current RN State:**
- active-session-id in app-db exists
- Not used to gate TTS playback
- May auto-speak from any session

**Implementation:**
1. Ensure active-session-id updates on navigation
2. Add check in TTS response handler
3. Only auto-speak if response session == active session
4. Allow manual speak for inactive sessions

**Files to modify:**
- frontend/src/voice_code/events/websocket.cljs - Add active session check
- frontend/src/voice_code/views/conversation.cljs - Set active on focus

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Auto-speak only for active session
- [ ] Background session responses don't auto-speak
- [ ] Manual tap-to-speak works for all sessions
- [ ] Active session updates on navigation

title: Session queue UI completeness
description:
iOS has both simple queue and priority queue features with full UI. Need to verify RN has complete queue management UI.

**iOS Queue Features:**
- Simple FIFO queue (position-based)
- Priority queue with 3-level sort
- Queue visibility toggle in settings
- Session card shows queue position
- Add/remove from queue buttons
- Priority picker (High/Medium/Low)
- Queue state persisted in CoreData

**Verify in RN:**
- Settings toggles exist (verified ✓)
- SessionInfo has priority queue section (verified ✓)
- Need to check: Queue display in DirectoryList/SessionList
- Need to check: Queue position persistence

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Queue position visible on session cards
- [ ] Add to queue works from SessionInfo
- [ ] Priority levels can be changed
- [ ] Queue state persists across app restarts

description:
iOS displays a 'Queue' section in the directory list when queue-enabled setting is true. This shows sessions that are in the regular queue (FIFO ordering). The RN directory list only shows Recent and project directories, missing the Queue section.

Implementation needed:
1. Add Queue section to DirectoryList when queue-enabled is true
2. Subscribe to sessions with queue-position set
3. Sort by queue position ascending
4. Show 'Remove from Queue' action in SessionInfo

Files to modify:

- frontend/src/voice_code/subs.cljs - Add :sessions/in-queue subscription

title: MRU (most-recently-used) command sorting
description:
iOS has CommandSorter that sorts commands by most-recently-used order. The RN command menu shows commands in static order from backend.

**iOS Features:**
- CommandSorter tracks MRU order
- Persists MRU list to UserDefaults
- Commands sorted by recency of use
- Groups also sorted by MRU of their children

**Current RN State:**
- Commands displayed in order received from backend
- No MRU tracking or persistence

**Implementation:**
- Track command execution timestamps in app-db
- Persist MRU list to AsyncStorage
- Sort commands by last-used time in subscription
- Apply sorting at display time, not storage

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Recently executed commands appear at top
- [ ] MRU order persists across app restarts
- [ ] New commands appear in default position
- [ ] Groups sorted by most recent child command

title: Fix message parsing for Claude JSONL format
description:
Fixed the :sessions/handle-history event handler to properly parse Claude JSONL messages:

1. Handle nested message structure with {:message {:role "...", :content ...}}
2. Extract text from content arrays for assistant messages [{:type "text", :text "..."}]
3. Filter out internal messages (queue-operation, summary, etc.) that don't have user/assistant roles

This fixes the Conversation view which was showing empty message bubbles and render errors.
close_reason: Closed

title: [UI Bug] Connection error displays as [object Object]
description:
## Steps to Reproduce
1. Connect to backend with an error state
2. View Settings screen

## Expected
Error field shows readable message

## Actual
Shows '[object Object]'

## Fix
Extract .-message from error object before storing

  - Sends session_deleted to backend

## Acceptance Criteria
- Add swipe-to-delete to session rows in SessionList
- Use react-native-swipeable-item or similar
- Clean up draft on delete
- Send session_deleted WebSocket message
- Mark session as is-user-deleted locally

title: Render loop detection for performance monitoring
description:
iOS ConversationView has sophisticated render loop detection that tracks renders per second and alerts when excessive rendering occurs. This helps identify performance issues during development.

## iOS Implementation
- ConversationView.swift lines 15-41, 123
- RenderTracker tracks renders per second
- Alerts when >60 renders/second detected
- Configurable minimum threshold

## Acceptance Criteria
- Create render loop detector hook
- Track component renders per second
- Log warning when threshold exceeded (e.g. >60/sec)
- Optional: Show dev-mode indicator for excessive rendering
- Use in ConversationView and other heavy components

title: Recipe orchestration UI in conversation toolbar
description:
iOS ConversationView shows active recipe status in the toolbar with step count and exit option. This is essential for recipe workflow visibility.

description:
iOS has ClipboardUtility for copying text with haptic feedback. RN has no copy functionality.

**iOS Features:**

- Haptic feedback on copy (success notification)
- Visual confirmation (toast/overlay)

**Current RN State:**

- No clipboard integration
- Users cannot copy Claude responses

**Implementation:**
1. Use @react-native-clipboard/clipboard
2. Add copy buttons/actions to messages
3. Add copy actions to session info
4. Show confirmation feedback
5. Optional: haptic feedback on iOS

**Files to modify:**
- frontend/src/voice_code/views/conversation.cljs - Add message copy
- frontend/src/voice_code/views/session_info.cljs - Add copy actions
- Add clipboard utility namespace

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Long-press or button copies message
- [ ] Session ID/directory copyable
- [ ] Visual feedback on copy
- [ ] Haptic feedback on iOS (nice-to-have)

title: Fix test to use Claude JSONL message format
description: Updated handle-session-history-test to use the actual Claude JSONL format that the backend sends (with nested :message, :type, :uuid fields instead of flat :role, :text, :id). This aligns the test with the implementation.
close_reason: Test updated to use Claude JSONL format - all 82 tests passing

title: Auto-updating relative timestamps
description:
iOS uses TimelineView to automatically update relative timestamps ('2 hours ago') as time passes. The RN app calculates relative time on render but doesn't auto-update.

**iOS Features:**
- RelativeTimeText component with auto-updating
- Uses TimelineView for periodic updates
- Timestamps update without user interaction

**Current RN State:**
- format-relative-time function calculates once on render
- Timestamps become stale without navigation

**Implementation:**
- Create useRelativeTime hook with setInterval
- Update every 60 seconds for minute-level precision
- Clean up interval on unmount
- Consider using react-native's InteractionManager to avoid animation jank

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Timestamps in DirectoryList update automatically
- [ ] Timestamps in SessionList update automatically
- [ ] No performance degradation from timer updates
- [ ] Interval cleaned up properly on unmount

description:
iOS has a full voice picker that shows all available TTS voices filtered to Premium+Enhanced quality. The RN settings currently shows voice identifier but has TODO for actual voice picker.

title: Audio session management - respect silent mode
description:
iOS has DeviceAudioSessionManager that respects the hardware silent/vibrate switch. RN TTS always plays regardless of device state.

**iOS Features:**
- Two audio session modes: ambient (respects silent) and playback (forces)
- User setting: 'Silence speech on vibrate'
- Configures AVAudioSession category appropriately
- SpokenAudio mode for optimal voice playback
- Background audio support with keep-alive timer

**Current RN State:**
- Basic react-native-tts usage
- No audio session configuration
- Ignores device silent mode
- No background audio support

**Implementation:**
1. Configure native audio session via bridge or library
2. Add setting for 'respect silent mode'
3. Check device ringer state before TTS
4. Integrate with voice.cljs TTS functions

**Files to modify:**
- frontend/src/voice_code/voice.cljs - Audio session config
- May need native module for iOS audio session

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] TTS respects device silent switch when setting enabled
- [ ] TTS plays through when setting disabled
- [ ] Setting persisted with other settings

title: Settings persistence across app restarts
description:
RN settings stored only in app-db (volatile memory) and lost on app restart. iOS uses UserDefaults for persistence.

**iOS Persisted Settings:**
- Server URL and port
- Voice identifier
- Silent mode respect flag
- Playback when locked
- Notification preferences
- Queue settings (enabled, priority-based)
- Resource storage location
- Max message size
- Custom system prompt

**Current RN State:**
- Settings in app-db (:settings key)
- No persistence layer
- All settings reset to defaults on restart

**Implementation:**
1. Use @react-native-async-storage/async-storage
2. Create persistence.cljs functions for settings
3. Load settings on app init
4. Save settings on change (debounced)
5. Migrate existing db.cljs settings structure

**Files to modify:**
- frontend/src/voice_code/persistence.cljs - Add settings persistence
- frontend/src/voice_code/core.cljs - Load settings on init
- frontend/src/voice_code/events/core.cljs - Save on settings change

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Settings persist across app restarts
- [ ] Settings load on app init before UI renders
- [ ] Changes saved with debouncing (300ms)
- [ ] Migration path for existing users
close_reason:
Settings persistence implemented. Changes:
1. core.cljs now calls :app/initialize after :initialize-db to load persisted settings on startup
2. settings.cljs uses :settings/save instead of :settings/update to persist changes
3. auth.cljs uses :settings/save for server config persistence
4. Added settings-save-event-test to persistence_test.cljs

title: [UI Bug] App shows blank white screen after restart
description:
## Symptom
After app restart (make rn-restart), app shows blank white screen and doesn't load.

## Environment

- Metro is not responding on port 8081

description:
iOS SessionInfoView includes git branch in session export when available. This provides more context about the session.

## iOS Implementation

- Uses date formatter with medium date and short time

## Acceptance Criteria

- Format date with medium date and short time

title: [UI Bug] SessionList shows 'No Sessions' despite data being available in subscription
description:
## Description
SessionList and Conversation views show empty state despite subscriptions containing data.

## Affected Views
1. **SessionList** - Shows 'No Sessions' when subscription has 20 sessions
2. **Conversation** - Shows 'Start a Conversation' when subscription has 54 messages

## Root Cause
Likely Reagent reactivity issue with `r/reactify-component` wrapper used for React Navigation screens. The reactive context may not be properly established when components are created through React Navigation.

## Additional Finding
Messages in the subscription have nil `:text` and `:role` fields - possible data parsing issue from WebSocket message handling.

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Authenticate via REPL
2. Navigate to SessionList or Conversation
3. Observe empty state despite subscription returning data

## Technical Analysis
```clojure
;; Subscription returns correct data:
@(rf/subscribe [:sessions/for-directory "/path"]) ;; => 20 sessions
@(rf/subscribe [:messages/for-session id]) ;; => 54 messages

;; But components check (empty? sessions) or (empty? messages) => true
```

## Impact
P1 - Core functionality broken. Users cannot see sessions or messages.
close_reason: Fixed by converting all view components to Form-2 pattern. The root cause was subscriptions being read in the outer function scope of components wrapped with r/reactify-component for React Navigation. Moving subscriptions into the inner render function establishes proper Reagent reactive context.

title: Compaction confirmation dialog
description:
iOS shows an Alert confirmation before compacting a session. The RN implementation triggers compaction immediately without confirmation.

Implementation needed:
1. Show Alert.alert before compaction

3. Show warning if recently compacted (like iOS)
4. Loading state on button during compaction

Files to modify:

title: Message truncation UI with View Full button
description:
iOS shows truncated messages with 'View Full' button for long content. RN has basic truncation but no expansion UI.

**iOS Features:**

**Current RN State:**

**Implementation:**
1. Add message length check (e.g., > 500 chars)
2. Show truncated preview with '...'
3. Add 'View Full' touchable text

5. Track expanded state per message

**Files to modify:**
- frontend/src/voice_code/views/conversation.cljs - Add truncation UI

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Long messages truncated with indicator
- [ ] 'View Full' button visible for truncated
- [ ] Tapping expands or shows full content
- [ ] Copy functionality for full content

title: Auto-scroll toggle in conversation view
description:
iOS has an auto-scroll toggle button in the conversation toolbar. RN auto-scrolls but has no toggle.

**iOS Features:**
- Toggle button in conversation toolbar
- Icon changes based on state (arrow down vs lock)
- When disabled, user can scroll freely
- When enabled, scrolls to bottom on new messages
- State persisted per-session

**Current RN State:**
- Always auto-scrolls to bottom
- No toggle UI
- Cannot read earlier messages while new ones arrive

**Implementation:**
1. Add toggle button to conversation toolbar

title: Kill session button to terminate Claude process
description:
iOS has a red 'X' button in the toolbar to kill/terminate the current Claude process. RN has no kill functionality.

**iOS Features:**
- Red X button visible when session is processing
- Sends 'kill' message to backend
- Claude CLI process terminated immediately
- Session unlocked after kill
- Useful for runaway or stuck operations

**Backend Protocol:**
- Sends: {type: 'kill', session_id: '...'}
- Receives: {type: 'session_killed', session_id: '...'}

**Implementation:**
1. Add kill button to conversation toolbar (red X icon)
2. Show only when session is locked/processing
3. Send kill WebSocket message
4. Handle session_killed response
5. Unlock session on kill confirmation

**Files to modify:**
- frontend/src/voice_code/views/conversation.cljs - Add kill button
- frontend/src/voice_code/events/websocket.cljs - Add kill event and handler

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Kill button visible when session processing
- [ ] Tapping sends kill message
- [ ] Session unlocks after kill confirmed
- [ ] Appropriate feedback shown to user

title: [UI Bug] DirectoryList session count not displayed - clj->js key conversion issue
description:
## Steps to Reproduce
1. Open the app
2. View the Projects (DirectoryList) screen
3. Observe each directory item shows 'sessions' without a number

## Expected Behavior
Each directory item should show the session count, e.g., '3 sessions' or '1 session'

## Actual Behavior
Each directory item just shows 'sessions' with no count

## Screenshot
/tmp/test-screenshot-1.png

## Root Cause
In directory_list.cljs, the render-item function uses:
```clojure
:session-count (.-sessionCount item)
```

But `clj->js` converts `{:session-count 5}` to JavaScript object with key `"session-count"` (preserves kebab-case as string), NOT `sessionCount`.

## Fix
Change the property access from:
```clojure
:session-count (.-sessionCount item)
:last-modified (.-lastModified item)
```
to:
```clojure
:session-count (aget item "session-count")
:last-modified (aget item "last-modified")
```

Or use `:keywordize-keys` option when converting.

## Additional Context
- File: frontend/src/voice_code/views/directory_list.cljs
- Function: directory-list-view, render-item callback
- Same issue likely affects last-modified display
close_reason: Closed

title: Session deletion should notify backend
description:
iOS sends a 'session_deleted' WebSocket message to the backend when a session is deleted. The RN implementation only marks the session as deleted locally and persists it, but doesn't notify the backend.

The iOS implementation:
1. Creates/updates CDUserSession record with deleted flag
2. Unsubscribes from session
3. Sends session_deleted message to backend
4. Session removes from UI

Current RN implementation only does steps 1 and 4. Steps 2 and 3 are missing.

Files to modify:
- frontend/src/voice_code/events/core.cljs - Add WS message to :sessions/delete event
close_reason: Session deletion now notifies backend and unsubscribes from session

title: [UI Bug] Screen frozen on CommandExecution - navigation/auth state changes don't trigger re-render
description:
## Description
The UI becomes completely frozen on the CommandExecution screen and does not respond to any navigation or authentication state changes.

## Steps to Reproduce
1. App is showing CommandExecution screen (from previous command)
2. Authenticate via REPL
3. Attempt to navigate to any screen via `navigate!` or `.reset`
4. Navigation state changes (verified via `.getState`) but UI doesn't update

## Observed Behavior
- Navigation state shows `{:index 2, :routes [DirectoryList, Settings, DirectoryList]}`
- UI continues to show CommandExecution screen (not in routes array!)
- Setting `authenticated? false` doesn't show auth-view
- `.reset` with new routes doesn't update display

## Expected Behavior
- Navigation should update displayed screen
- Auth state change should switch between auth-view and NavigationContainer

## Technical Details
- nav-ref.getState() returns correct state
- re-frame subscriptions return correct values
- React component tree not re-rendering
- re-frame warning visible: "no :event handler registered for: (...)"

## Impact
P0 - App unusable when this state occurs. Requires app restart to recover.
close_reason: Fixed by converting all view components to Form-2 pattern (inner render function) so subscriptions are read inside a reactive context. This ensures React Navigation screens properly re-render when re-frame subscriptions change.

title: Session refresh button in conversation toolbar

3. Show loading indicator while refreshing
4. Disable button while session is locked or already refreshing

description:

- UIViewControllerRepresentable wrapper for SwiftUI
- Camera permissions handling with user feedback

- Parses QR codes containing API key and server info

**Required for RN:**
1. Install react-native-camera or react-native-vision-camera
2. Create QRScannerView component with camera preview
3. Implement QR code detection and parsing
4. Handle camera permissions with graceful fallback
5. Extract API key and server info from QR payload
6. Navigate to auth completion on successful scan

**Files to create:**
- frontend/src/voice_code/views/qr_scanner.cljs
- May need native module bridging

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Camera preview displays in scanner view
- [ ] QR codes detected and parsed automatically
- [ ] API key extracted and stored securely
- [ ] Permission denied shows helpful message
- [ ] Cancel button returns to auth view

title: Fix command history showing 'Unknown command'
description: The command history view was showing 'Unknown command' for all entries because the render-item function was using incorrect JavaScript property access patterns. When clj->js converts Clojure maps, kebab-case keywords like :shell-command become hyphenated strings 'shell-command', not snake_case 'shell_command' or camelCase 'shellCommand'. Fixed by using aget with correct hyphenated key names.
close_reason: Fixed in command_history.cljs by using aget with hyphenated keys instead of JS property access with snake_case/camelCase

title: Add missing WebSocket message handlers for protocol parity
description:
The RN app is missing handlers for several WebSocket message types that the iOS app handles:

1. **session_name_inferred** - Handle when Claude suggests a session name automatically
2. **infer_name_error** - Handle when name inference fails  
3. **session_ready** - Handle when a session becomes ready
4. **session_killed** - Handle when a session is killed/deleted from backend

These are needed for full protocol parity with iOS app.

Files to modify:
- frontend/src/voice_code/events/websocket.cljs - Add message handlers
- frontend/src/voice_code/db.cljs - Possibly add state for tracking inference status

Reference: @STANDARDS.md WebSocket protocol
close_reason: Implemented handlers for session_ready, session_killed, session_name_inferred, and infer_name_error. Added 4 new tests (99 total tests, 378 assertions).

title: Simulator-testable: Implement and test draft persistence
description:
This feature can be fully tested on iOS simulator - SQLite works on simulator.

Implement draft persistence to storage:
1. Add draft save function to persistence.cljs (use settings table or new drafts table)
2. Add debounced save effect (300ms) when draft changes
3. Load drafts on app startup and populate ui/drafts state
4. Clear draft from storage when message sent
5. Handle draft cleanup when session deleted

Testing approach:
- Type a draft message in conversation
- Force-quit and restart app
- Navigate back to same session
- Verify draft text restored
- Send message, verify draft cleared
- Restart app, verify draft gone
- Take screenshots to verify

Parent: react-native-jta
close_reason: Implemented and tested draft persistence - save/load/delete drafts with SQLite storage and debouncing.

title: Draft persistence to storage
description:
iOS persists message drafts to UserDefaults with debouncing so they survive app restart. React Native has drafts in memory only. Implement:
1. Save drafts to AsyncStorage or SQLite settings table
2. Debounce saves (300ms like iOS)
3. Load drafts on app startup
4. Clear draft from storage when message sent
5. Handle draft cleanup on session delete

Files to modify:
- frontend/src/voice_code/persistence.cljs - Add draft save/load functions
- frontend/src/voice_code/events/core.cljs - Add debounced draft persistence
- frontend/src/voice_code/core.cljs - Load drafts on app init
close_reason: Implemented draft persistence with debounced SQLite writes

title: Background audio keep-alive for TTS
description:
iOS uses a silent audio placeholder to keep the audio session alive for TTS playback when app is in background or screen is locked. React Native needs:
1. Generate or bundle silent audio file
2. Configure audio session for background playback
3. Play silent audio on 25-second timer when TTS is active
4. Stop keep-alive when TTS completes
5. Respect 'continue playback when locked' setting

Files to modify:
- frontend/src/voice_code/voice.cljs - Add keep-alive timer logic
- ios/VoiceCodeMobile/Info.plist - Add audio background mode if not present

title: Simulator-testable: Implement and test message truncation
description:
This feature can be fully tested on iOS simulator - no physical device needed.

Implement message truncation for large responses:
1. Add truncation logic: first 250 chars + '...[N chars truncated]...' + last 250 chars
2. Add expanded state per message
3. Add 'Show more'/'Show less' toggle button for truncated messages
4. Keep full text in state, only truncate for rendering

Testing approach:
- Use REPL to add a very long message (>1000 chars) to a session
- Verify truncation appears with character count
- Tap 'Show more', verify full text displays
- Tap 'Show less', verify truncation returns
- Take screenshots to verify UI

Parent: react-native-fxc
close_reason: Implemented message truncation with Show more/less toggle in conversation.cljs and added tests

title: Render loop detection for performance monitoring
description:
iOS monitors for performance issues by detecting render loops (>50 renders/sec warning). React Native needs:
1. Add render counter to key components
2. Track renders per second
3. Log warning when threshold exceeded
4. Consider using React DevTools Profiler integration
5. Add performance metrics to debug logs view

This helps identify infinite re-render bugs during development.

Files to modify:
- frontend/src/voice_code/performance.cljs - New file for performance monitoring
- frontend/src/voice_code/views/conversation.cljs - Add render tracking

title: Android platform testing and fixes

Android project exists in frontend/android/ but has not been tested. Steps:

2. Verify app builds and launches on emulator
3. Test all screens: Auth, DirectoryList, SessionList, Conversation, Settings, CommandMenu, CommandExecution, Resources, Recipes
4. Test WebSocket connection and authentication
5. Test native modules: SQLite, Keychain equivalents
6. Document any platform-specific issues and fix them

Note: Voice features may need Android-specific testing after iOS verification is complete.

title: Simulator-testable: Implement and test auto-speak responses
description:
This feature can be fully tested on iOS simulator - TTS works on simulator!

Implement auto-speak for Claude responses:
1. Add :auto-speak-responses setting to db.cljs defaults
2. Add toggle in Settings view under Audio Playback section
3. In websocket response handler, check setting and dispatch :voice/speak if enabled
4. Ensure TTS doesn't speak when app is in voice input mode (prevent feedback)

Testing approach:
- Enable auto-speak in Settings
- Send a prompt via text mode
- Verify TTS speaks the response automatically
- Disable auto-speak, verify silence on next response
- Test that voice input mode suppresses auto-speak

Note: Push notifications require physical device, track separately.
